content.heading: Comparisons — asking yes or no questions

body.text: Every decision a program makes comes down to a question with a yes or no answer. Is the score high enough? Is the player alive? Is this the right answer? Comparison operators ask those questions. The answer is always a boolean: true or false.

body.desc: AND — both conditions must be true for the whole thing to be true
OR — at least one condition must be true
NOT — flips true to false and false to true

lines: // OR — at least one must be true

body.label: === not ==
body.text: Always use === (three equals signs) to check if things are equal. Using == (two equals signs) lets JavaScript change types before comparing, which causes bugs. Stick to === and you'll avoid a whole class of problems.

exercises.title: Predict true or false
tasks: Before running anything — write true or false for each line in the starter code.
Then run it and check your answers.
For any wrong ones: explain why the result is what it is.
exercises.hint: Work through each comparison one at a time. Don't rush — this is the most important skill in programming.

exercises.title: Build your own comparisons
tasks: Write a comparison that checks if playerScore is at least 70. Log it.
Write a comparison that checks if lives is NOT 0. Log it.
Write a comparison that checks if playerScore is above 50 AND lives is above 0. Log it.
Write a comparison that checks if playerName equals 'Sam' OR 'Alex'. Log it.
Challenge: write a comparison that's true only when the player has NOT won (score below 100).
exercises.hint: For the OR name check: playerName === 'Sam' || playerName === 'Alex'

checks.label: at least one comparison logged true

exercises.title: Logical operator puzzles
tasks: Predict the result of each expression before running.
After checking, write one sentence explaining the rule for &&, one for ||, and one for !.
Then come up with your own example of a real-world situation for each operator.
exercises.hint: For &&: both sides must be true. For ||: either side just needs to be true. For !: it just flips it.

challenges.title: The age check is always wrong
challenges.description: This check should be true when age is 13 or older. But it's returning false for age = 13. One character is wrong.
challenges.hint: The condition is 'older than 13' not '13 or older'. One extra character fixes it.
challenges.explanation: > means strictly greater than — 13 > 13 is false. To include 13 itself, you need >= (greater than OR equal to).

challenges.title: The password check always passes
challenges.description: This login check should only pass when BOTH the username AND password are correct. Right now it passes even with the wrong password.
challenges.hint: The logic requires BOTH to be correct. Which operator requires both sides to be true?

checks.name: isLoggedIn
checks.label: isLoggedIn is false when password is wrong

challenges.explanation: || means OR — if either condition is true, the whole thing is true. Since username === 'admin' IS true, it doesn't even check the password. Change || to && so BOTH must be true.

challenges.title: The name check never matches
challenges.description: The check should be true when the name is 'Alex'. It's never true even when you set name to 'Alex'.
challenges.hint: There are TWO problems here. Look at the operator AND look at the capitalization.
challenges.explanation: Two bugs: (1) 'Alex' !== 'alex' — strings are case-sensitive. (2) Using == instead of ===. Fix both: change == to === and change 'alex' to 'Alex'.
